text: Large Language Models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence designed to understand and generate human-like text. They work by predicting the next word in a sentence based on patterns learned from vast amounts of training data, such as books, articles, and websites. To get helpful responses from an LLM, users often use prompt engineering—crafting clear and specific instructions. These models can write, summarize, and even answer questions. However, they have limits; they can produce incorrect or biased information, and they lack true understanding or common sense.
